skill.name: convex
skill.description: Routes broad Convex development requests to the most relevant Convex workflow skill, including setup, auth, components, migrations, and performance review.
skill.summary: Routes general Convex requests to the right specialized workflow skill.
skill.author: get-convex

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged command, network, and weak-crypto patterns in SKILL.md. Review found no malicious intent or prompt injection: the command is a documented Convex CLI recommendation, the links point to Convex guidance, and the weak-crypto hits are false positives from Markdown/front matter context.

medium_findings.title: External CLI Command Recommendation
medium_findings.description: The skill recommends running npx convex ai-files install. This is a legitimate Convex setup command, but it can fetch and execute package code through npm and modify project guidance files, so users should review the command before running it.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command is explicit in a bash block and repeated inline. The surrounding text frames it as official Convex setup guidance, which lowers maliciousness but keeps a real execution risk.

low_findings.title: External Documentation Links
low_findings.description: The skill links to Convex AI documentation and a Convex-hosted rules text file. These network references are relevant to the stated purpose and do not show data exfiltration or suspicious domains.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Both URLs are visible Markdown links connected to Convex guidance. No credential, file, or telemetry data is sent to those URLs in the skill text.

low_findings.title: False Positive Static Pattern Matches
low_findings.description: Static analysis reported weak cryptography at lines 3 and 27 and shell execution at lines 41 through 44. Review found front matter or routing text only, with inline skill names rather than executable commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines contain metadata, prose, or names of other Convex skills. They do not contain cryptographic operations or executable shell syntax.

dangerous_patterns.title: npx Command in Skill Instructions
dangerous_patterns.description: The instructions include an npx command for installing Convex AI files. This is not malicious by itself, but npx can execute downloaded package code and should be treated as an external command risk.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The command is plainly present and asks users to run package-managed tooling. The command appears aligned with the skill purpose, so the risk is operational rather than malicious.

content.user_title: Route Convex Tasks to the Right Skill
content.value_statement: Convex users can lose time choosing the right guidance for broad app requests. This skill routes each request to the most relevant Convex workflow skill and setup guidance.
actual_capabilities: Identifies when a general Convex request needs a more specific workflow skill.
Recommends installing or refreshing Convex AI guidance when project guidance is missing or stale.
Routes new app and Convex setup work to the quickstart workflow.
Routes authentication, component, migration, and performance tasks to specialized Convex skills.
Provides official Convex documentation links for AI guidance setup.
limitations: Does not implement Convex app code by itself.
Does not verify that Convex AI files were installed successfully.
Depends on other Convex skills being available in the environment.
May require user confirmation before running external CLI commands.

use_cases.title: Choose a Convex workflow
use_cases.description: Route a broad Convex request to setup, auth, component, migration, or performance guidance.

use_cases.title: Refresh project AI guidance
use_cases.description: Identify when Convex AI guidance is absent or stale and recommend the managed installation path.

use_cases.title: Triage incoming Convex tasks
use_cases.description: Convert an underspecified Convex task into the next specialized skill or documentation step.

prompt_templates.title: Pick the right Convex skill
prompt_templates.prompt: I need help with a Convex app, but I am not sure which workflow to use. Route me to the right Convex skill.

prompt_templates.title: Start a new Convex app
prompt_templates.prompt: I am adding Convex to a web app. Decide which Convex workflow skill should handle the setup.

prompt_templates.title: Plan a Convex migration
prompt_templates.prompt: I need to change my Convex schema and migrate existing data. Route this request and list the first checks.

prompt_templates.title: Triage mixed Convex work
prompt_templates.prompt: Review these Convex tasks: auth setup, reusable component work, and slow queries. Choose the best workflow order and skill for each task.

output_examples.input: I need Convex help, but I only know that my app needs login.
output_examples.output: The skill routes the request to the Convex authentication setup workflow and notes that project AI guidance should be current first.

output_examples.input: My Convex queries are slow after recent data growth.
output_examples.output: The skill routes the request to the Convex performance audit workflow and avoids using a general setup path.

output_examples.input: We need to add Convex and create initial project guidance.
output: Recommend refreshing Convex AI files first.
Route the main implementation work to the Convex quickstart workflow.
Use official Convex guidance links for setup context.

best_practices: Install or refresh managed Convex AI files before detailed implementation work.
Switch to a specialized Convex skill when the user goal clearly matches one.
Ask one focused clarifying question when the request could match several workflows.
anti_patterns: Do not stay in this routing skill after a specific Convex workflow is identified.
Do not copy rules manually when the managed Convex AI files installer is suitable.
Do not run external CLI commands without explaining their purpose and impact.

faq.question: What does this skill do?
faq.answer: It routes broad Convex requests to the most relevant specialized Convex workflow skill.

faq.question: Does it write Convex code?
faq.answer: No. It selects the right workflow and setup guidance before implementation starts.

faq.question: Why does it mention npx?
faq.answer: It recommends the Convex CLI installer for managed AI guidance files when guidance is missing or stale.

faq.question: Is the npx command required?
faq.answer: No. It is the preferred starting point, but users can review documentation or use the fallback link.

faq.question: Which tools support this skill?
faq.answer: The report lists Claude, Codex, and Claude Code as supported tools.

faq.question: When should I use a different Convex skill?
faq.answer: Use a specific Convex skill directly when the task is clearly about setup, auth, components, migrations, or performance.
